question: Were all restrictions in eligibility crieria based on study characteristics appropriate (e.g. date, sample size, study quality, outcomes measured)? If yes, indicate which study characteristic was an inclusion/exclusion criteria
answers: Only case reports or case series studies which provided individual patient information were included

question: Were any restrictions in eligibility criteria based on sources of info appropriate (e.g. publication status or format, language, availability of data)?
answers:
Inclusion criteria included English abstracts and articles pertaining to patients with primary scrotal melanoma.


Non-English articles and reply-to-author articles were excluded.

question: Were biases in primary studies minimal or addressed in the synthesis?
Did the interpretation of findings address all of the concerns identified in Domains 1 to 4?
answers: As scrotal melanoma is an extremely rare entity, this posed challenges and limitations to this study. First, this study is limited with respect to a small sample size. There were only 23 reported cases, of which 20 cases met criteria for inclusion. Another limitation is that all the data were derived from case reports, putting the literature at risk for potential bias. However, as this disease is so rare, there is currently no other means to gather information on scrotal melanoma.
